name: Boots on the Ground
description: Anonymous peer support for First Responders, by First Responders. Serving police, fire, EMS, and corrections personnel.

name: Farmer Wellness Initiative
description: 24/7 confidential mental health support for Ontario farmers, farm families, and employees.

name: Fem'aide
description: The provincial helpline for Francophone women dealing with violence. Offers support, referrals and information 24/7.

name: Indian Residential Schools Crisis Line
description: A 24/7 crisis line for former residential school students and their families. Provides emotional support related to residential school trauma.

name: LGBT YouthLine
description: A Queer, Trans, Two-Spirit* youth-led organization that affirms and supports the experiences of youth (29 and under) across Ontario.

name: Support Services for Male Survivors of Sexual Abuse
description: A 24/7 multilingual crisis and referral line for male survivors of sexual abuse or assault in Ontario.

name: Métis Nation of Ontario Crisis Line
description: Mental health and addictions support for Métis citizens in Ontario. Entry point to MNO healing and wellness programs.

name: MMIWG Crisis Line
description: A national 24/7 support line for those impacted by missing and murdered Indigenous women, girls, and 2SLGBTQQIA+ people.

name: Naseeha Mental Health
description: Provides a confidential helpline for young Muslims to receive immediate, anonymous, and confidential support.

name: National Overdose Response Service (NORS)
description: A 24/7 virtual safe consumption hotline. An operator stays on the line while you use drugs to prevent fatal overdoses.

name: ONTX - Ontario Online & Text Crisis Services
description: A provincial text and chat service for individuals experiencing distress, providing confidential support from trained responders.

name: Sexual Assault/Domestic Violence Navigation Line
description: Helps victims navigate the healthcare system after an assault. Staffed by nurses who connect survivors to SA/DV Treatment Centres for forensic evidence collection and medical care.

name: Seniors Safety Line
description: A 24/7 confidential line for seniors suffering from abuse (physical, mental, sexual, neglect, or financial).

name: VAC Assistance Service
description: A 24/7 confidential counseling line for Veterans, Canadian Armed Forces members, RCMP members, and their families.

name: Victim Support Line (VSL)
description: A provincial 24/7 information and support line for victims of crime, including survivors of sexual assault and domestic violence.

name: BounceBack Ontario
description: A guided self-help program for adults and youth (15+) with mild-to-moderate depression and anxiety. Includes telephone coaching and online workbooks.

name: Cancer Information Helpline
description: A national toll-free service answering questions about cancer types, treatments, side effects, and prevention. Connects patients to local support services.

name: Ontario Caregiver Helpline
description: A 24/7 dedicated helpline for caregivers in Ontario, offering information, assessment, and referral to local services and support groups.

name: Diabetes Canada Information & Support
description: Information and support for people living with diabetes, including access to the Ontario Monitoring for Health Program for financial assistance with supplies.

name: Jordan's Principle Call Centre
description: A 24/7 federal helpline ensuring First Nations children get the health, social, and educational services they need without delays.

name: Lung Health Line
description: Connects callers directly with Certified Respiratory Educators for guidance on asthma, COPD, and other lung conditions.

name: Sexual Health Infoline Ontario (SHILO)
description: Anonymous and confidential counselling and information on sexual health, including STIs, HIV/AIDS, birth control, pregnancy, and sexual orientation.

name: Advocacy Centre for the Elderly (ACE)
description: A specialty legal clinic providing legal services to seniors (60+) in Ontario. Focuses on elder law issues like long-term care, elder abuse, and health care consent.

name: Employment Standards Information Centre
description: Provides general information about Ontario's Employment Standards Act (ESA), covering minimum wage, hours of work, overtime, vacation, and termination pay.

name: HIV & AIDS Legal Clinic Ontario (HALCO)
eligibility: People living with HIV/AIDS in Ontario
description: Provides free legal services for people living with HIV/AIDS in Ontario. Covers privacy, human rights, immigration, and social assistance.

name: Human Rights Legal Support Centre (HRLSC)
description: Provides free legal advice and assistance to individuals who have experienced discrimination under the Ontario Human Rights Code.

name: Injured Workers Community Legal Clinic (IWC)
description: Specializes in representing injured workers in WSIB disputes. Handles denied claims and benefit issues.

name: Justice for Children and Youth (JFCY)
description: Protects the legal rights of minors (under 18) and homeless youth (under 25) in Ontario. Covers education, family, criminal, and social welfare law.

name: Landlord's Self-Help Centre
description: A specialty legal clinic providing information, summary advice, and referrals to small-scale landlords in Ontario.

name: Legal Aid Ontario
description: Ontario’s primary public legal assistance provider. Offers certificates for full representation, duty counsel in courts, and summary legal advice by phone for criminal, family, and immigration law.

name: Landlord and Tenant Board Contact Centre
description: Provides information about the Residential Tenancies Act and LTB procedures. Staff can explain forms and the eviction process but cannot provide legal advice.

name: Ontario Legal Information Centre
description: Offers a free 30-minute meeting with a lawyer to anyone in Ontario, regardless of income. Provides general legal information and referrals for any area of law.

name: Office of the Worker Adviser (OWA)
description: Provides free legal advice and representation to non-unionized injured workers in WSIB cases and workers facing health and safety reprisals.

name: Steps to Justice
description: A comprehensive online guide to law in Ontario. Features step-by-step information and a Live Chat service to help users find legal answers.

name: Workers Health & Safety Legal Clinic
description: Assists non-unionized workers who face punishment or firing for raising health and safety concerns. Focuses on reprisals and unsafe work refusals.

name: WSIB (Workplace Safety & Insurance Board)
description: Administers Ontario's no-fault workers' compensation system. Handles claims for workplace injuries and illnesses.

name: Arthritis Society Canada
description: Telephone and email support for people affected by arthritis. Trained volunteers provide information on arthritis types, treatment, pain management, and local resources.

name: Heart and Stroke Foundation
eligibility: Anyone seeking heart or stroke information
description: Provides evidence-based information on heart disease and stroke, including prevention, risk factors, recovery, and healthy living resources.

name: Hospice Palliative Care Ontario (HPCO)
eligibility: Patients, families, and caregivers seeking palliative care
description: Provincial helpline for palliative care information and navigation. Helps individuals, families, and caregivers find hospice programs, palliative care units, pain management services, and bereavement support.

name: MS Knowledge Network (MS Canada)
description: National MS information and navigation service. Connects callers with MS Navigators who provide evidence-based information on multiple sclerosis, disease management, treatment, and local services.

name: Parkinson Canada
description: Helpline for people living with Parkinson's disease, caregivers, and healthcare providers. Offers information about symptoms, treatment options, community resources, and support programs.

name: Talk Tobacco
eligibility: Indigenous peoples seeking tobacco cessation support
description: Culturally appropriate quit-smoking and vaping support for Indigenous peoples (First Nations, Inuit, Métis). Offers free, confidential counselling with respect for traditional tobacco use.

name: Aboriginal Legal Services (ALS)
description: Provincial legal clinic serving Indigenous peoples across Ontario. Provides legal advice, representation, police complaints assistance, child welfare support (Giiwedin Anang Council), and Gladue report writing.

name: Consumer Protection Ontario
description: Provincial helpline providing free information on consumer rights and unfair business practices. Assists with door-to-door sales, car repairs, home renovations, collection agencies, contracts, and consumer complaints.

name: Law Society Referral Service
description: Free referral to a lawyer or licensed paralegal who will provide a free 30-minute consultation. Operated by the Law Society of Ontario to connect people with legal professionals.

name: Nishnawbe-Aski Legal Services (NALSC)
eligibility: Indigenous peoples in NAN territory (financial testing applies)
description: Legal aid clinic serving Nishnawbe Aski Nation (NAN) communities in Northern Ontario. Provides legal advice, certificates, and restorative justice programs including Talking Together.
